Our expert hornet removal services safely eliminate nests from homes, businesses, and outdoor areas throughout this waterfront Queens neighborhood. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive hornet control with pricing ranging from $350 to $550 depending on nest size and location.

localContext: College Point's tree-lined streets, older single-family homes near areas like Malba, and commercial zones along College Point Boulevard create ideal nesting spots for hornets under eaves, in attics, and around the many waterfront properties. The neighborhood's parks like Macneil Park and proximity to water can attract hornets seeking shelter, making professional removal essential for homeowners and businesses alike.

faqs.question: How much does hornet removal cost in College Point?
faqs.answer: Hornet removal in College Point typically costs between $350 and $550, depending on the nest size, location accessibility, and severity of the infestation. Ground-level nests are generally less expensive than those in high or hard-to-reach areas like attics or tall trees. We provide free estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in College Point?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day hornet removal service throughout College Point, Queens. Most hornet nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours. We understand hornets pose an immediate safety risk, especially to those with allergies, so we prioritize emergency calls and work efficiently to eliminate the threat.

faqs.question: Is hornet removal safe for my family and pets in College Point?
faqs.answer: Yes, our hornet removal methods are completely safe for your family and pets. We use EPA-approved products and professional techniques that target only the hornets and their nest. Our technicians take precautions to protect your property and will advise you on any temporary safety measures during the treatment process.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, wasps, and yellow jackets?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than wasps and yellow jackets, typically building large, enclosed paper nests in trees or under eaves. Yellow jackets often nest in the ground or wall voids and are more aggressive. Wasps build open, umbrella-shaped nests. All three can sting multiple times, but hornets deliver the most painful sting and are more defensive of their nests, requiring professional removal for safety.
